input.description: a question that needs gathering and weighing several sources

output.description: a source-cited answer that passed a grounding check

nodes.prompt: Break the question into 3-6 independent research angles. Write each angle as a SELF-CONTAINED search query that names the specific subject of the question, so it stands on its own without the original question. Output ONLY a JSON array of these query strings, e.g. ["health benefits of X", "risks and side effects of X"]. No other text.

Investigate the self-contained research query you were given. Make AT MOST 2-3 web searches and read AT MOST 3-4 sources total, then STOP — do not re-fetch a URL you already read or keep hunting for more. Return concise findings, each claim followed by its source (a URL or title). Cite specifically; never invent a source or summarize vaguely. If sources are thin, say so rather than over-searching.

Using the gathered findings, write one coherent, well-structured answer to the original question. Keep every non-trivial claim tied to a source from the findings. Where the findings are thin, say so rather than guessing.

Check the answer for grounding against the gathered findings. Give a one-line reason, then end your reply with EXACTLY one of these labels on its own final line:
GROUNDED  — every non-trivial claim is supported by a cited source in the findings.
MISSING   — some claim has no supporting source; more evidence must be gathered.
MISUSED   — some claim contradicts or overstates what its source actually says; rewrite from the existing findings.
